template_lines: You are a philatelic cataloging assistant. From the stamp image and the dealer context below, produce structured data for THIS stamp only.
Suggested primary key for the stamp row (use this exact value as stamp.stamp_id when you emit a stamp object): __STAMP_SUGGESTED_ID__
Image file name: __IMAGE_BASENAME____FILE_MISMATCH_GAP__
Commit summary line (from uploader):
__FIRST_LINE__
Full structured parameters from the upload form (JSON):
__META_JSON__
Dealer target listing price (JSON; may be null): __TARGET_JSON__
Dealer notes and hints (JSON; may be null): __NOTES_JSON__
=== CONSOLIDATED JSON SCHEMA (xFrame) ===

The following is the project's consolidated JSON Schema (root schema with $defs per entity). Every object you output for an entity MUST conform to the matching $defs entry and respect required properties, enums, and types.
__CONSOLIDATED_SCHEMA_JSON__
=== OUTPUT CONTRACT ===
Return ONE JSON object and nothing else (no markdown fences, no commentary before or after).
Top-level keys MUST be entity names that appear as keys in the schema's $defs (e.g. stamp, country, listing, issue_series, catalog_system, stamp_catalog_number, stamp_thematic_tag, thematic_tag).
Each top-level value MUST be a JSON array of record objects for that entity, even if there is only one record (e.g. "stamp": [ { ... } ]).

Include only entities and fields you can justify from the image and the context above; omit entire entity keys you have no data for. Do not invent catalog numbers unless reasonably inferred; prefer uncertainty via omitted fields or notes fields where the schema allows.

If the dealer provided a target price, emit a listing row (see listing in $defs) with stamp_id equal to the suggested id above, with target_price and listing_currency when you can infer currency from context (default USD if only a bare number is given).
All string enum values must match the schema exactly (e.g. condition_quality, centering_grade).
Foreign keys (stamp_id, country_id, etc.) must reference ids you also emit in the same object or that are plausible reference slugs consistent with the image/notes.
